summary: Coder v6 correctly addresses all critical and major findings from v1. Exception handling wraps all 6 behavior methods consistently. Relative prefix bug fixed with trailing separator guard. Move.Overwrite deletes existing dir before move. Delete non-empty dir returns clear error. Null guards added. Copy file-to-dir resolved. 6 new targeted tests. Two minor observations remain but nothing blocking. Recommend merge.

findings.issue: ResolveDestination (file-to-existing-directory) is only used by Copy, not Move. A user who writes 'move file.txt to existing_dir/' would get the file at the directory path, not inside it. Copy handles this correctly but Move doesn't.
findings.impact: Inconsistent behavior between copy and move for the same source/destination pattern. Low frequency — most users specify a file destination.
findings.suggestion: Apply ResolveDestination in Move as well, or document the difference.

findings.issue: Relative returns string.Empty when the path IS the root directory. This means ToString() returns empty string for root paths, which could surprise PLang users who interpolate %path% in output.
findings.impact: Cosmetic. User writes 'write %path%' for a root directory and gets empty string. Very rare scenario.
findings.suggestion: Consider returning '.' or '/' for the root case. Not blocking.
